001/*
002 * Licensed to the Apache Software Foundation (ASF) under one or more
003 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
004 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.
005 * The ASF licenses this file to You under the Apache License, Version 2.0
006 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
007 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
008 *
009 *      http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
010 *
011 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
012 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
013 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
014 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
015 * limitations under the License.
016 */
017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server;
018
019import static java.lang.annotation.ElementType.*;
020import static java.lang.annotation.RetentionPolicy.*;
021
022import java.lang.annotation.*;
023
024import org.apache.juneau.bean.swagger.*;
025import org.apache.juneau.commons.*;
026import org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.*;
027import org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.*;
028import org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.*;
029import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.converter.*;
030import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.guard.*;
031import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.matcher.*;
032import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.servlet.*;
033import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.swagger.*;
034
035/**
036 * Identifies a REST PUT operation Java method on a {@link RestServlet} implementation class.
037 *
038 * <p>
039 * This is a specialized subtype of <c><ja>{@link RestOp @RestOp}(method=<jsf>PUT</jsf>)</c>.
040 *
041 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
042 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestOpAnnotatedMethods">@RestOp-Annotated Method Basics</a>
043
044 * </ul>
045 */
046@Target(METHOD)
047@Retention(RUNTIME)
048@Inherited
049@AnnotationGroup(RestOp.class)
050public @interface RestPut {
051
052   /**
053    * Specifies whether this method can be called based on the client version.
054    *
055    * <p>
056    * The client version is identified via the HTTP request header identified by
057    * {@link Rest#clientVersionHeader() @Rest(clientVersionHeader)} which by default is <js>"Client-Version"</js>.
058    *
059    * <p>
060    * This is a specialized kind of {@link RestMatcher} that allows you to invoke different Java methods for the same
061    * method/path based on the client version.
062    *
063    * <p>
064    * The format of the client version range is similar to that of OSGi versions.
065    *
066    * <p>
067    * In the following example, the Java methods are mapped to the same HTTP method and URL <js>"/foobar"</js>.
068    * <p class='bjava'>
069    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 2.0.
070    *    // Note that this also matches 2.0.1.</jc>
071    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"2.0"</js>)
072    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method1()  {...}
073    *
074    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 1.1, but less than 2.0.</jc>
075    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[1.1,2.0)"</js>)
076    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method2()  {...}
077    *
078    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is less than 1.1.</jc>
079    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[0,1.1)"</js>)
080    *    <jk>public</jk> Object method3()  {...}
081    * </p>
082    *
083    * <p>
084    * It's common to combine the client version with transforms that will convert new POJOs into older POJOs for
085    * backwards compatibility.
086    * <p class='bjava'>
087    *    <jc>// Call this method if Client-Version is at least 2.0.</jc>
088    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"2.0"</js>)
089    *    <jk>public</jk> NewPojo newMethod()  {...}
090    *
091    *    <jc>// Call this method if X-Client-Version is at least 1.1, but less than 2.0.</jc>
092    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/foobar"</js>, clientVersion=<js>"[1.1,2.0)"</js>)
093    *    <ja>@MarshalledConfig</ja>(swaps=NewToOldSwap.<jk>class</jk>)
094    *    <jk>public</jk> NewPojo oldMethod() {
095    *       <jk>return</jk> newMethod();
096    *    }
097    *
098    * <p>
099    * Note that in the previous example, we're returning the exact same POJO, but using a transform to convert it into
100    * an older form.
101    * The old method could also just return back a completely different object.
102    * The range can be any of the following:
103    * <ul>
104    *    <li><js>"[0,1.0)"</js> = Less than 1.0.  1.0 and 1.0.0 does not match.
105    *    <li><js>"[0,1.0]"</js> = Less than or equal to 1.0.  Note that 1.0.1 will match.
106    *    <li><js>"1.0"</js> = At least 1.0.  1.0 and 2.0 will match.
107    * </ul>
108    *
109    *
110    * @return The annotation value.
111    */
112   String clientVersion() default "";
113
114   /**
115    * Supported content media types.
116    *
117    * <p>
118    * Overrides the media types inferred from the parsers that identify what media types can be consumed by the resource.
119    *
120    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
121    *    <li class='note'>
122    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
123    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
124    * </ul>
125    *
126    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
127    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestOpContext#getSupportedContentTypes()}
128    * </ul>
129    *
130    * @return The annotation value.
131    */
132   String[] consumes() default {};
133
134   /**
135    * Class-level response converters.
136    *
137    * <p>
138    * Associates one or more {@link RestConverter converters} with this method.
139    *
140    *
141    * @return The annotation value.
142    */
143   Class<? extends RestConverter>[] converters() default {};
144
145   /**
146    * Enable debug mode.
147    *
148    * <p>
149    * Enables the following:
150    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
151    *    <li>
152    *       HTTP request/response bodies are cached in memory for logging purposes.
153    *    <li>
154    *       Request/response messages are automatically logged.
155    * </ul>
156    *
157    * <ul class='values'>
158    *    <li><js>"true"</js> - Debug is enabled for all requests.
159    *    <li><js>"false"</js> - Debug is disabled for all requests.
160    *    <li><js>"conditional"</js> - Debug is enabled only for requests that have a <c class='snippet'>Debug: true</c> header.
161    *    <li><js>""</js> (or anything else) - Debug mode is inherited from class.
162    * </ul>
163    *
164    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
165    *    <li class='note'>
166    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
167    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
168    * </ul>
169    *
170    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
171    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestContext#getDebugEnablement()}
172    * </ul>
173    *
174    * @return The annotation value.
175    */
176   String debug() default "";
177
178   /**
179    * Default <c>Accept</c> header.
180    *
181    * <p>
182    * The default value for the <c>Accept</c> header if not specified on a request.
183    *
184    * <p>
185    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
186    *
187    * @return The annotation value.
188    */
189   String defaultAccept() default "";
190
191   /**
192    * Default character encoding.
193    *
194    * <p>
195    * The default character encoding for the request and response if not specified on the request.
196    *
197    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
198    *    <li class='note'>
199    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
200    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
201    * </ul>
202    *
203    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
204    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#defaultCharset}
205    * </ul>
206    *
207    * @return The annotation value.
208    */
209   String defaultCharset() default "";
210
211   /**
212    * Default <c>Content-Type</c> header.
213    *
214    * <p>
215    * The default value for the <c>Content-Type</c> header if not specified on a request.
216    *
217    * <p>
218    * This is a shortcut for using {@link #defaultRequestHeaders()} for just this specific header.
219    *
220    * @return The annotation value.
221    */
222   String defaultContentType() default "";
223
224   /**
225    * Default request attributes.
226    *
227    * <p>
228    * Specifies default values for request attributes if they're not already set on the request.
229    *
230    * <p>
231    * Affects values returned by the following methods:
232    *    <ul>
233    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttribute(String)}.
234    *       <li class='jm'>{@link RestRequest#getAttributes()}.
235    *    </ul>
236    *
237    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
238    * <p class='bjava'>
239    *    <jc>// Defined via annotation resolving to a config file setting with default value.</jc>
240    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo=bar"</js>, <js>"Baz: $C{REST/myAttributeValue}"</js>})
241    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
242    *
243    *       <jc>// Override at the method level.</jc>
244    *       <ja>@RestGet</ja>(defaultRequestAttributes={<js>"Foo: bar"</js>})
245    *       <jk>public</jk> Object myMethod() {...}
246    *    }
247    * </p>
248    *
249    * </ul>
250    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
251    *    <li class='note'>
252    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
253    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
254    * </ul>
255    *
256    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>  *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#defaultRequestAttributes()}
257    * </ul>
258    *
259    * @return The annotation value.
260    */
261   String[] defaultRequestAttributes() default {};
262
263   /**
264    * Specifies default values for form-data parameters.
265    *
266    * <p>
267    * Strings are of the format <js>"name=value"</js>.
268    *
269    * <p>
270    * Affects values returned by {@link RestRequest#getFormParam(String)} when the parameter is not present on the
271    * request.
272    *
273    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
274    * <p class='bjava'>
275    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestFormData={<js>"foo=bar"</js>})
276    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPut(<ja>@FormData</ja>(<js>"foo"</js>) String <jv>foo</jv>)  {...}
277    * </p>
278    *
279    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
280    *    <li class='note'>
281    *       You can use either <js>':'</js> or <js>'='</js> as the key/value delimiter.
282    *    <li class='note'>
283    *       Key and value is trimmed of whitespace.
284    *    <li class='note'>
285    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
286    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
287    * </ul>
288    *
289    * @return The annotation value.
290    */
291   String[] defaultRequestFormData() default {};
292
293   /**
294    * Default request headers.
295    *
296    * <p>
297    * Specifies default values for request headers if they're not passed in through the request.
298    *
299    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
300    * <p class='bjava'>
301    *    <jc>// Assume "text/json" Accept value when Accept not specified</jc>
302    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestHeaders={<js>"Accept: text/json"</js>})
303    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPut()  {...}
304    * </p>
305    *
306    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
307    *    <li class='note'>
308    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
309    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
310    * </ul>
311    *
312    *
313    * @return The annotation value.
314    */
315   String[] defaultRequestHeaders() default {};
316
317   /**
318    * Specifies default values for query parameters.
319    *
320    * <p>
321    * Strings are of the format <js>"name=value"</js>.
322    *
323    * <p>
324    * Affects values returned by {@link RestRequest#getQueryParam(String)} when the parameter is not present on the request.
325    *
326    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
327    * <p class='bjava'>
328    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultRequestQueryData={<js>"foo=bar"</js>})
329    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPut(<ja>@Query</ja>(<js>"foo"</js>) String <jv>foo</jv>)  {...}
330    * </p>
331    *
332    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
333    *    <li class='note'>
334    *       You can use either <js>':'</js> or <js>'='</js> as the key/value delimiter.
335    *    <li class='note'>
336    *       Key and value is trimmed of whitespace.
337    *    <li class='note'>
338    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
339    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
340    * </ul>
341    *
342    * @return The annotation value.
343    */
344   String[] defaultRequestQueryData() default {};
345
346   /**
347    * Default response headers.
348    *
349    * <p>
350    * Specifies default values for response headers if they're not overwritten during the request.
351    *
352    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
353    * <p class='bjava'>
354    *    <jc>// Assume "text/json" Accept value when Accept not specified</jc>
355    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/*"</js>, defaultResponseHeaders={<js>"Content-Type: text/json"</js>})
356    *    <jk>public</jk> String doPut()  {...}
357    * </p>
358    *
359    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
360    *    <li class='note'>
361    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
362    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
363    * </ul>
364    *
365    *
366    * @return The annotation value.
367    */
368   String[] defaultResponseHeaders() default {};
369
370   /**
371    * Optional description for the exposed API.
372    *
373    * <p>
374    * This description is used in the following locations:
375    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
376    *    <li>
377    *       The value returned by {@link Operation#getDescription()} in the auto-generated swagger.
378    *    <li>
379    *       The <js>"$RS{operationDescription}"</js> variable.
380    *    <li>
381    *       The description of the method in the Swagger page.
382    * </ul>
383    *
384    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
385    *    <li class='note'>
386    *       Corresponds to the swagger field <c>/paths/{path}/{method}/description</c>.
387    *    <li class='note'>
388    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
389    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
390    * </ul>
391    *
392    * @return The annotation value.
393    */
394   String[] description() default {};
395
396   /**
397    * Specifies the compression encoders for this method.
398    *
399    * <p>
400    * Encoders are used to enable various kinds of compression (e.g. <js>"gzip"</js>) on requests and responses.
401    *
402    * <p>
403    * This value overrides encoders specified at the class level using {@link Rest#encoders()}.
404    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.encoders.EncoderSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
405    *
406    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
407    * <p class='bjava'>
408    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that handles GZIP compression.</jc>
409    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
410    *       encoders={
411    *          GzipEncoder.<jk>class</jk>
412    *       }
413    *    )
414    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
415    *
416    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also use a custom encoder.</jc>
417    *       <ja>@RestPut</ja>(
418    *          encoders={
419    *             EncoderSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, MyEncoder.<jk>class</jk>
420    *          }
421    *       )
422    *       <jk>public void</jk> doPut(MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) {
423    *          ...
424    *       }
425    *    }
426    * </p>
427    *
428    * <p>
429    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link EncoderSet} bean via
430    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Bean @Bean(name="encoders")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
431    *
432    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
433    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerEncoders">Encoders</a>
434    * </ul>
435    *
436    * @return The annotation value.
437    */
438   Class<? extends Encoder>[] encoders() default {};
439
440   /**
441    * Method-level guards.
442    *
443    * <p>
444    * Associates one or more {@link RestGuard RestGuards} with this method.
445    *
446    *
447    * @return The annotation value.
448    */
449   Class<? extends RestGuard>[] guards() default {};
450
451   /**
452    * Method matchers.
453    *
454    * <p>
455    * Associates one more more {@link RestMatcher RestMatchers} with this method.
456    *
457    * <p>
458    * Matchers are used to allow multiple Java methods to handle requests assigned to the same URL path pattern, but
459    * differing based on some request attribute, such as a specific header value.
460    *
461    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
462    *    <li class='jac'>{@link RestMatcher}
463    * </ul>
464    *
465    * @return The annotation value.
466    */
467   Class<? extends RestMatcher>[] matchers() default {};
468
469   /**
470    * The maximum allowed input size (in bytes) on HTTP requests.
471    *
472    * <p>
473    * Useful for alleviating DoS attacks by throwing an exception when too much input is received instead of resulting
474    * in out-of-memory errors which could affect system stability.
475    *
476    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
477    * <p class='bjava'>
478    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(
479    *       maxInput=<js>"100M"</js>
480    *    )
481    * </p>
482    *
483    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
484    *    <li class='note'>
485    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
486    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
487    * </ul>
488    *
489    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
490    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#maxInput}
491    * </ul>
492    *
493    * @return The annotation value.
494    */
495   String maxInput() default "";
496
497   /**
498    * Specifies the parsers for converting HTTP request bodies into POJOs for this method.
499    *
500    * <p>
501    * Parsers are used to convert the content of HTTP requests into POJOs.
502    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework parsers can be used in this setting.
503    * <br>The parser selected is based on the request <c>Content-Type</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
504    * using a best-match algorithm:
505    * <ul class='javatree'>
506    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Parser#getMediaTypes()}
507    * </ul>
508    *
509    * <p>
510    * This value overrides parsers specified at the class level using {@link Rest#parsers()}.
511    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ParserSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
512    *
513    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
514    * <p class='bjava'>
515    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can consume JSON and HTML.</jc>
516    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
517    *       parsers={
518    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
519    *          HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
520    *       }
521    *    )
522    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
523    *
524    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also consume XML.</jc>
525    *       <ja>@RestPut</ja>(
526    *          parsers={
527    *             ParserSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
528    *          }
529    *       )
530    *       <jk>public void</jk> doPut(MyBean <jv>bean</jv>) {
531    *          ...
532    *       }
533    *    }
534    * </p>
535    *
536    * <p>
537    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link ParserSet} bean via
538    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Bean @Bean(name="parsers")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
539    *
540    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
541    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
542    * </ul>
543    *
544    * @return The annotation value.
545    */
546   Class<?>[] parsers() default {};
547
548   /**
549    * Optional path pattern for the specified method.
550    *
551    * <p>
552    * Appending <js>"/*"</js> to the end of the path pattern will make it match any remainder too.
553    * <br>Not appending <js>"/*"</js> to the end of the pattern will cause a 404 (Not found) error to occur if the exact
554    * pattern is not found.
555    *
556    * <p>
557    * The path can contain variables that get resolved to {@link org.apache.juneau.http.Path @Path} parameters.
558    *
559    * <h5 class='figure'>Examples:</h5>
560    * <p class='bjava'>
561    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/myurl/{foo}/{bar}/{baz}/*"</js>)
562    * </p>
563    * <p class='bjava'>
564    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/myurl/{0}/{1}/{2}/*"</js>)
565    * </p>
566    *
567    * <p>
568    * If you do not specify a path name, then the path name is inferred from the Java method name.
569    *
570    * <h5 class='figure'>Example:</h5>
571    * <p class='bjava'>
572    *    <jc>// Path is assumed to be "/foo".</jc>
573    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>
574    *    <jk>public void</jk> foo() {...}
575    * </p>
576    *
577    * <p>
578    * Note that you can also use {@link #value()} to specify the path.
579    *
580    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
581    *    <li class='ja'>{@link org.apache.juneau.http.Path}
582    * </ul>
583    *
584    * @return The annotation value.
585    */
586   String[] path() default {};
587
588   /**
589    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#problemDetails() @Rest(problemDetails)}.
590    *
591    * <p>
592    * When set on a {@code @RestPut} method, this value takes precedence over the resource-level
593    * {@code @Rest(problemDetails)} attribute for this operation. Tri-state semantics:
594    *
595    * <ul class='values'>
596    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; enable problem-details responses for this operation.
597    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; disable problem-details responses for this operation (overrides an
598    *       opted-in resource).
599    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherit from {@link Rest#problemDetails()}.
600    * </ul>
601    *
602    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
603    *    <li class='note'>
604    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
605    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
606    * </ul>
607    *
608    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
609    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#problemDetails()}
610    * </ul>
611    *
612    * @return The annotation value.
613    */
614   String problemDetails() default "";
615
616   /**
617    * Per-operation observability opt-in / opt-out control.
618    *
619    * <p>
620    * Overrides the resource-level {@link Rest#observability()} setting for this specific operation.
621    *
622    * <ul class='values'>
623    *    <li><js>"true"</js> &mdash; strict opt-in; requires a wired observability backend.
624    *    <li><js>"false"</js> &mdash; explicit opt-out; short-circuits the observability block.
625    *    <li><js>""</js> (default) &mdash; inherits the resource-level setting.
626    * </ul>
627    *
628    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
629    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#observability()}
630    * </ul>
631    *
632    * @return The annotation value.
633    * @since 10.0.0
634    */
635   String observability() default "";
636
637   /**
638    * Per-operation override for {@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor() @Rest(asyncCompletionExecutor)}.
639    *
640    * <p>
641    * Names the {@link java.util.concurrent.Executor} bean used to route completion callbacks for this
642    * specific operation through a dedicated thread pool. Empty string (default) inherits from
643    * {@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor()}.
644    *
645    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
646    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#asyncCompletionExecutor()}
647    *    <li class='ja'>{@link Rest#asyncCompletionExecutor()}
648    * </ul>
649    *
650    * @return The annotation value.
651    * @since 10.0.0
652    */
653   String asyncCompletionExecutor() default "";
654
655   /**
656    * Per-operation metric name override. Empty string (default) uses the recorder's own name derivation.
657    *
658    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
659    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#metricName()}
660    * </ul>
661    *
662    * @return The annotation value.
663    * @since 10.0.0
664    */
665   String metricName() default "";
666
667   /**
668    * Per-operation metric tags. Format: comma-separated {@code key=value} pairs.
669    * Empty string (default) means no additional tags.
670    *
671    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
672    *    <li class='ja'>{@link RestOp#metricTags()}
673    * </ul>
674    *
675    * @return The annotation value.
676    * @since 10.0.0
677    */
678   String metricTags() default "";
679
680   /**
681    * Supported accept media types.
682    *
683    * <p>
684    * Overrides the media types inferred from the serializers that identify what media types can be produced by the resource.
685    *
686    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
687    *    <li class='note'>
688    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
689    *       (e.g. <js>"$S{mySystemProperty}"</js>).
690    * </ul>
691    *
692    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
693    *    <li class='jm'>{@link RestOpContext#getSupportedAcceptTypes()}
694    * </ul>
695    *
696    * @return The annotation value.
697    */
698   String[] produces() default {};
699
700   /**
701    * Allowed serializer session option keys for this operation (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
702    *
703    * <p>
704    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Serializer-Options"</js>
705    * header or <js>"juneauSerializerOptions"</js> query parameter.
706    * Merged on top of resource-level {@link Rest#allowedSerializerOptions()} values.
707    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to replace rather than extend the resource-level list.
708    *
709    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
710    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
711    * </ul>
712    *
713    * @return The annotation value.
714    */
715   String[] allowedSerializerOptions() default {};
716
717   /**
718    * Allowed parser session option keys for this operation (ordered merge, prefix {@code -key} removes a key).
719    *
720    * <p>
721    * Comma-delimited list of session property keys that clients may send via the <js>"X-Juneau-Parser-Options"</js>
722    * header or <js>"juneauParserOptions"</js> query parameter.
723    * Merged on top of resource-level {@link Rest#allowedParserOptions()} values.
724    * Use {@link #noInherit()} to replace rather than extend the resource-level list.
725    *
726    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
727    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions#safe-properties">Session Options - Safe Properties</a>
728    * </ul>
729    *
730    * @return The annotation value.
731    */
732   String[] allowedParserOptions() default {};
733
734   /**
735    * Property names for which less-derived contributions are NOT inherited.
736    *
737    * <p>
738    * Accepted values: {@code "allowedSerializerOptions"}, {@code "allowedParserOptions"}.
739    * Prevents the named property from inheriting values from the enclosing {@code @Rest} annotation.
740    * The {@code noInherit} attribute itself is never inherited.
741    *
742    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
743    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/SessionOptions">Session Options</a>
744    * </ul>
745    *
746    * @return The annotation value.
747    */
748   String[] noInherit() default {};
749
750   /**
751    * Role guard.
752    *
753    * <p>
754    * An expression defining if a user with the specified roles are allowed to access this method.
755    *
756    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
757    * <p class='bjava'>
758    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
759    *
760    *       <ja>@RestPut</ja>(
761    *          path=<js>"/foo"</js>,
762    *          roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
763    *       )
764    *       <jk>public</jk> Object doPut() {
765    *       }
766    *    }
767    * </p>
768    *
769    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
770    *    <li class='note'>
771    *       Supports any of the following expression constructs:
772    *       <ul>
773    *          <li><js>"foo"</js> - Single arguments.
774    *          <li><js>"foo,bar,baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments.
775    *          <li><js>"foo | bar | baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, pipe syntax.
776    *          <li><js>"foo || bar || baz"</js> - Multiple OR'ed arguments, Java-OR syntax.
777    *          <li><js>"fo*"</js> - Patterns including <js>'*'</js> and <js>'?'</js>.
778    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, ampersand syntax.
779    *          <li><js>"fo* &amp;&amp; *oo"</js> - Multiple AND'ed arguments, Java-AND syntax.
780    *          <li><js>"fo* || (*oo || bar)"</js> - Parenthesis.
781    *       </ul>
782    *    <li class='note'>
783    *       AND operations take precedence over OR operations (as expected).
784    *    <li class='note'>
785    *       Whitespace is ignored.
786    *    <li class='note'>
787    *       <jk>null</jk> or empty expressions always match as <jk>false</jk>.
788    *    <li class='note'>
789    *       If patterns are used, you must specify the list of declared roles using {@link #rolesDeclared()}.
790    *    <li class='note'>
791    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
792    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
793    *    <li class='note'>
794    *       When defined on parent/child classes and methods, ALL guards within the hierarchy must pass.
795    * </ul>
796    *
797    *
798    * @return The annotation value.
799    */
800   String roleGuard() default "";
801
802   /**
803    * Declared roles.
804    *
805    * <p>
806    * A comma-delimited list of all possible user roles.
807    *
808    * <p>
809    * Used in conjunction with {@link #roleGuard()} is used with patterns.
810    *
811    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
812    * <p class='bjava'>
813    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource <jk>extends</jk> BasicRestServlet {
814    *
815    *       <ja>@RestPut</ja>(
816    *          path=<js>"/foo"</js>,
817    *          rolesDeclared=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN,ROLE_READ_WRITE,ROLE_READ_ONLY,ROLE_SPECIAL"</js>,
818    *          roleGuard=<js>"ROLE_ADMIN || (ROLE_READ_WRITE &amp;&amp; ROLE_SPECIAL)"</js>
819    *       )
820    *       <jk>public</jk> Object doPut() {
821    *       }
822    *    }
823    * </p>
824    *
825    *
826    * @return The annotation value.
827    */
828   String rolesDeclared() default "";
829
830   /**
831    * Specifies the serializers for marshalling POJOs into response bodies for this method.
832    *
833    * <p>
834    * Serializer are used to convert POJOs to HTTP response bodies.
835    * <br>Any of the Juneau framework serializers can be used in this setting.
836    * <br>The serializer selected is based on the request <c>Accept</c> header matched against the values returned by the following method
837    * using a best-match algorithm:
838    * <ul class='javatree'>
839    *    <li class='jm'>{@link Serializer#getMediaTypeRanges()}
840    * </ul>
841    *
842    * <p>
843    * This value overrides serializers specified at the class level using {@link Rest#serializers()}.
844    * The {@link org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.SerializerSet.Inherit} class can be used to include values from the parent class.
845    *
846    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
847    * <p class='bjava'>
848    *    <jc>// Define a REST resource that can produce JSON and HTML.</jc>
849    *    <ja>@Rest</ja>(
850    *       serializers={
851    *          JsonParser.<jk>class</jk>,
852    *          HtmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
853    *       }
854    *    )
855    *    <jk>public class</jk> MyResource {
856    *
857    *       <jc>// Define a REST method that can also produce XML.</jc>
858    *       <ja>@RestPut</ja>(
859    *          parsers={
860    *             SerializerSet.Inherit.<jk>class</jk>, XmlParser.<jk>class</jk>
861    *          }
862    *       )
863    *       <jk>public</jk> MyBean doPut() {
864    *          ...
865    *       }
866    *    }
867    * </p>
868    *
869    * <p>
870    * For programmatic equivalents, contribute a {@link SerializerSet} bean via
871    * {@link org.apache.juneau.commons.inject.Bean @Bean(name="serializers")} (use methodScope to scope to specific operation methods).
872    *
873    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
874    *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/Marshalling">Marshalling</a>
875    * </ul>
876    *
877    * @return The annotation value.
878    */
879   Class<? extends Serializer>[] serializers() default {};
880
881   /**
882    * Optional summary for the exposed API.
883    *
884    * <p>
885    * This summary is used in the following locations:
886    * <ul class='spaced-list'>
887    *    <li>
888    *       The value returned by {@link Operation#getSummary()} in the auto-generated swagger.
889    *    <li>
890    *       The <js>"$RS{operationSummary}"</js> variable.
891    *    <li>
892    *       The summary of the method in the Swagger page.
893    * </ul>
894    *
895    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
896    *    <li class='note'>
897    *       Corresponds to the swagger field <c>/paths/{path}/{method}/summary</c>.
898    *    <li class='note'>
899    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
900    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
901    * </ul>
902    *
903    * @return The annotation value.
904    */
905   String summary() default "";
906
907   /**
908    * Provides swagger-specific metadata on this method.
909    *
910    * <p>
911    * Used to populate the auto-generated OPTIONS swagger documentation.
912    *
913    * <p>
914    * The format of this annotation is JSON when all individual parts are concatenated.
915    * <br>The starting and ending <js>'{'</js>/<js>'}'</js> characters around the entire value are optional.
916    *
917    * <h5 class='section'>Example:</h5>
918    * <p class='bjava'>
919    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(
920    *       path=<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>,
921    *
922    *       <jc>// Swagger info.</jc>
923    *       swagger={
924    *          <js>"parameters:["</js>,
925    *             <js>"{name:'propertyName',in:'path',description:'The system property name.'},"</js>,
926    *             <js>"{in:'body',description:'The new system property value.'}"</js>,
927    *          <js>"],"</js>,
928    *          <js>"responses:{"</js>,
929    *             <js>"302: {headers:{Location:{description:'The root URL of this resource.'}}},"</js>,
930    *             <js>"403: {description:'User is not an admin.'}"</js>,
931    *          <js>"}"</js>
932    *       }
933    *    )
934    * </p>
935    *
936    * <h5 class='section'>Notes:</h5><ul>
937    *    <li class='note'>
938    *       The format is <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/JuneauBeanSwagger2">juneau-bean-swagger-v2</a>.
939    *       <br>Multiple lines are concatenated with newlines.
940    *    <li class='note'>
941    *       The starting and ending <js>'{'</js>/<js>'}'</js> characters around the entire value are optional.
942    *    <li class='note'>
943    *       These values are superimposed on top of any Swagger JSON file present for the resource in the classpath.
944    *    <li class='note'>
945    *       Supports <a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/RestServerSvlVariables">SVL Variables</a>
946    *       (e.g. <js>"$L{my.localized.variable}"</js>).
947    * </ul>
948    *
949    * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
950    *    <li class='ja'>{@link OpSwagger}
951    *    <li class='jc'>{@link SwaggerProvider}
952    * </ul>
953    *
954    * @return The annotation value.
955    */
956   OpSwagger swagger() default @OpSwagger;
957
958   /**
959    * REST method path.
960    *
961    * <p>
962    * Can be used to provide a shortened form for the {@link #path()} value.
963    *
964    * <p>
965    * The following examples are considered equivalent.
966    * <p class='bjava'>
967    *    <jc>// Normal form</jc>
968    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(path=<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>)
969    *
970    *    <jc>// Shortened form</jc>
971    *    <ja>@RestPut</ja>(<js>"/{propertyName}"</js>)
972    * </p>
973    *
974    * @return The annotation value.
975    */
976   String value() default "";
977}
